allow:
Bash(csa debate "Review this skills migration plan for risks, missed considerations, and logical errors.

PLAN SUMMARY:
- 20 skills analyzed in ./skills/
- 12 categorized as PATTERN candidates \(workflow/orchestration to convert to skill-lang\)
- 5 categorized as GLOBAL \(standalone capabilities to keep in ~/.claude/skills/\)
- 3 categorized as DELETE \(redundant/superseded to remove\)

PATTERN candidates \(12\): ai-reviewed-commit, code-review, commit, csa-issue-reporter, csa-review, debate, mktd, mktsk, pr-codex-bot, sa, security-audit
GLOBAL keeps \(6\): csa, csa-async-debug, csa-doc-writing, csa-rust-dev, csa-security, csa-test-gen
DELETE candidates \(3\): csa-analyze, csa-cc, install-update-csa

KEY DECISIONS:
1. Should ai-reviewed-commit merge into commit pattern or stay separate?
2. Is deleting csa-cc safe given it routes 14 Claude Code sub-agents to CSA?
3. Is deleting csa-analyze safe given AGENTS.md rules already cover delegation?
4. Can skill-lang \(IF/FOR/Step/INCLUDE only, no WHILE/ELIF/expressions\) express all workflow logic?
5. Circular deps: commit references pr-codex-bot which references commit. How?
6. Should install-update-csa be deleted or moved to project docs?
7. Is 4-week timeline realistic for 11 patterns?

CONTEXT: skill-lang supports Step, IF/ELSE/ENDIF, FOR/IN/ENDFOR, INCLUDE, variables, hint lines \(Tool:/Tier:/OnFail:\). No WHILE, ELIF, expressions, or runtime condition evaluation. All 20 skills currently symlinked to global.")
Bash(for pattern in debate csa-issue-reporter csa-review code-review ai-reviewed-commit commit mktd mktsk pr-codex-bot sa)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(config\): harden default resource limits for heavyweight tools

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(sandbox\): apply profile defaults when no project config exists

When no .csa/config.toml is present, resolve_sandbox_options now calls
default_sandbox_for_tool\(\) to apply profile-based resource limits for
heavyweight tools \(claude-code, gemini-cli\) instead of short-circuiting
with no isolation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(config,sandbox\): inherit heavyweight memory defaults when enforcement is set

P1: sandbox_memory_max_mb/swap used tool_resource_profile\(\) for fallback,
which returns Custom when only enforcement_mode is set. Custom profile
has memory_max_mb: None, silently skipping sandbox. Now uses inherent
\(runtime-based\) profile via default_profile\(\), matching the pattern
already used by tool_enforcement_mode\(\).

P2: DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS was 300 in pipeline.rs but config.rs
and init.rs use 120. Aligned to 120.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr create --base main --title "fix\(patterns\): add PATTERN.md fallback for legacy pattern layouts" --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Summary
- Adds `PATTERN.md` fallback in `resolve_pattern\(\)` for legacy weave-locked packages that don''t have `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` \(#199\)
- Priority: `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` > `PATTERN.md` \(new layout wins when both exist\)
- Updates error messages and manual-install guidance in `debate_cmd.rs` and `review_cmd.rs` to mention both file options

## Test plan
- [x] `resolve_pattern_falls_back_to_pattern_md` — verifies resolution from PATTERN.md when no skills/ dir exists
- [x] `resolve_pattern_skill_md_takes_priority` — verifies SKILL.md content returned when both files exist
- [x] All 20 pattern_resolver tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy -p cli-sub-agent` clean

Fixes #199

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)
EOF
\)")

Bash(env -u CLAUDECODE csa debate --rounds 3 "Adversarial review of TODO plan for issue #218 \(restore codex -c notify=[] for notification suppression\).

THE PLAN \(from .csa/plans/issue-218-codex-notify-suppression.md\):

## Background
Codex desktop notifications cannot be suppressed in CSA sub-agent mode because ACP unification refactoring removed codex''s native \\`-c ''notify=[]''\\` CLI parameter injection, replacing it with \\`CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1\\` env var, which codex itself does not recognize. Need to restore codex-specific \\`-c ''notify=[]''\\` parameter injection at the executor layer, while preserving \\`CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1\\` for future tools.

## Relevant Files
- \\`crates/csa-executor/src/executor.rs\\` — core modification point: \\`build_execute_in_command\(\)\\` and \\`append_model_args\(\)\\` \(Codex branch\)
- \\`crates/csa-executor/src/executor_build_cmd_tests.rs\\` — need new tests
- \\`crates/cli-sub-agent/src/pipeline_env.rs\\` — keep as-is \(CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1 logic preserved\)
- \\`crates/cli-sub-agent/src/pipeline.rs\\` — keep as-is \(suppress_notify call chain entry\)

## Phase 2 Implementation Approach
In \\`build_execute_in_command\(\)\\`, when \\`extra_env\\` contains \\`CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1\\`, inject \\`-c notify=[]\\` CLI arg for Codex executor. Check \\`extra_env\\` for the signal key after \\`append_yolo_args\(\)\\` but before \\`append_prompt_args\(\)\\`.

## Phase 3 Tests
- \\`test_build_execute_in_command_codex_notify_suppressed\\`
- \\`test_build_execute_in_command_codex_notify_not_suppressed\\`

## ARCHITECTURE CONTEXT:
- Pipeline layer: \\`build_merged_env\(\)\\` in pipeline_env.rs checks \\`config.should_suppress_notify\(tool\)\\` \(per-tool, default=true\), injects \\`CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1\\` into extra_env HashMap
- Executor layer: has NO direct access to config — only receives extra_env HashMap
- Codex already uses \\`-c model_reasoning_effort=...\\` in \\`append_model_args\(\)\\`
- \\`append_model_args\(\)\\` currently takes no extra_env parameter
- Claude-code: handles suppression natively via ACP transport recognizing CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY env var
- gemini-cli/opencode: receive the env var but native support is unclear

## EVALUATE THESE SPECIFIC QUESTIONS:
1\) Is using CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY env var as signal to inject -c notify=[] the right approach, or should executor access suppress_notify config directly? Consider: separation of concerns, executor''s current design \(no config access\), information flow.

2\) Is Phase 2''s approach of checking extra_env for signal clean architecture or a hack? extra_env was designed for environment propagation to child processes, not as a control channel for CLI arg injection decisions. Is this leaky abstraction?

3\) Are there other tools needing native suppression beyond env var? gemini-cli? opencode? Does the plan miss anything?

4\) Is test coverage sufficient? Only 2 tests planned. What about: -c notify=[] combined with -c model_reasoning_effort interaction? ACP vs Legacy transport paths? suppress_notify=false config?

5\) Risk of -c notify=[] conflicting with other -c parameters already injected \(model_reasoning_effort\)?

Provide your analysis as: APPROVE / REVISE / REJECT with detailed reasoning for each question.")

Bash(env -u CLAUDECODE csa debate --rounds 3 "Adversarial review of TODO plan for issue #218 \(restore codex -c notify=[] for notification suppression\). The plan is at .csa/plans/issue-218-codex-notify-suppression.md.

CONTEXT FOR DEBATERS:
- The plan proposes: in build_execute_in_command\(\) and/or build_command\(\), when extra_env contains CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1, inject -c notify=[] as a CLI arg for the Codex executor.
- Currently: pipeline_env.rs reads suppress_notify config and sets CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY=1 in extra_env HashMap. Executor receives extra_env but never reads config directly.
- Codex already uses -c for model_reasoning_effort \(e.g. -c model_reasoning_effort=low\). This is the ONLY current -c usage.
- The executor builds Command objects; extra_env is injected via inject_env\(\) which sets env vars on the Command.
- There are ~20 existing tests in executor_build_cmd_tests.rs covering arg structure, env injection, etc.
- The plan proposes 2 new tests: one for suppressed, one for not-suppressed.

DEBATE QUESTIONS:
1\) Is using CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY env var in extra_env as a signal to inject -c notify=[] the right approach? Or should the executor access suppress_notify config directly? The current architecture has executor as a pure command builder that doesn''t know about ProjectConfig.
2\) Is Phase 2''s approach of checking extra_env for CSA_SUPPRESS_NOTIFY clean architecture or a hack? extra_env is meant for env vars to pass to child process, not as a signaling mechanism between pipeline and executor layers.
3\) Are there any other tools \(gemini-cli, claude-code, opencode\) that also need native notification suppression beyond the env var?
4\) Is the proposed test coverage \(2 tests\) sufficient? What about: multiple -c args interaction, build_command vs build_execute_in_command paths, ACP path?
5\) Risk of -c notify=[] conflicting with -c model_reasoning_effort? Does codex handle multiple -c flags correctly?

Output APPROVE/REVISE/REJECT with detailed reasons.")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(debate\): enforce tool enablement in same-model fallback

Filter resolve_same_model_fallback by project-enabled tools instead of
all_known_tools\(\). Check parent tool enablement before returning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(run\): mark executed session as seed candidate instead of newest

Use the actual session ID from execution path when updating
is_seed_candidate, not a global sort of all sessions.

Both the fork genealogy update and seed candidate marking now use
the executed_session_id obtained from execute_with_session_and_meta,
fixing a bug where `csa run --session <existing-id>` could tag an
unrelated newer session, skewing seed selection and LRU eviction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(migrate\): handle missing versions section in weave.lock

Replace expect\(\) with graceful fallback when weave.lock has only
package entries and no versions section, preventing panic on
csa migrate after weave install.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(run\): reset fork session ID on tool failover

Clear effective_session_arg alongside fork_resolution when failover
switches tools, preventing stale session reuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(run,scheduler,session\): graceful auto-fork fallback, seed readiness filter, unique section paths

Fall back to cold start when automatic fork resolution fails instead
of aborting. Filter seed candidates by provider_session_id for native
fork readiness. Deduplicate section filenames to prevent content overwrites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(pr-codex-bot\): add pre-merge rebase step and user-prompted round limit

Add Step 10.5 to rebase fix commits into logical groups before merge.
Replace auto-merge at round limit with user prompt offering merge,
continue, or abort options.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): check round cap before review trigger and reset counter on continue

Move MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS check to top of loop to prevent unnecessary
review triggers at cap. Reset REVIEW_ROUND when user chooses continue
so another full batch of rounds actually runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): block on post-rebase review and fix SKILL.md merge-base reference

Add explicit poll-and-wait after rebase force-push review trigger.
Update SKILL.md to use git merge-base instead of local main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): enforce round-limit user choice and post-rebase review verdict

Handle A/B/C options at round limit instead of unconditional exit.
Gate merge on post-rebase bot review verdict when response received.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): add commit creation in rebase and enforce fallback fix routing

Add selective staging and commit steps between soft reset and force push
in Step 10.5. Make fallback review failure actually route to fix cycle
instead of just logging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): fix skip-on-failure bypass, keyword heuristic, and pathspec guards

Use state variable instead of exit code for fallback review gate.
Replace keyword grep with P1/P2 badge detection for review verdict.
Use dynamic file discovery instead of hard-coded pathspecs in rebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): make fallback fix path reachable and gate merge on rebase review

Add dedicated fix cycle in timeout branch for fallback review failures.
Ensure post-rebase review issues trigger fix cycle via correct gate variable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): gate rebase on clean review and use NUL-delimited paths

Add BOT_HAS_ISSUES check to Step 10.5 condition in workflow.toml.
Use -z/-0 flags for safe filename handling in rebase staging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(pr-codex-bot\): clear ROUND_LIMIT_REACHED on user merge/continue choice

Reset the flag in merge and continue branches so downstream steps
\(10.5, 11, 12\) are not blocked by stale round-limit gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(cli\): extract fork resolution logic from run_cmd.rs

Move ForkResolution, resolve_fork\(\), load_child_return_packet\(\), and
cleanup_pre_created_fork_session\(\) into new run_cmd_fork.rs module.
This reduces run_cmd.rs from 1549 to 1375 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(cli\): extract tool selection helpers from run_cmd.rs

Move resolve_last_session_selection, resolve_heterogeneous_candidates,
take_next_runtime_fallback_tool, resolve_slot_wait_timeout_seconds,
resolve_session_reference, and resolve_return_target_session_id into
new run_cmd_tool_selection.rs module. Reduces run_cmd.rs to 1260 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(cli\): extract project key helpers from pipeline.rs

Move slugify_identifier, project_key_from_path, project_key_from_git_remote,
strip_url_credentials, project_key_from_git_toplevel, and
resolve_memory_project_key to new pipeline_project_key module \(~124 lines\).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(session\): extract tests from state.rs to state_tests.rs

Reduces state.rs from 1016 to 383 lines \(below 800-line soft limit\)
by moving all unit tests to a separate file using #[path] pattern,
consistent with existing manager_tests.rs and soft_fork_tests.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(mcp-hub\): extract HTTP safety validation from registry.rs

Move validate_http_url, preflight_ssrf_check, parse_host_port,
is_ssrf_dangerous_ip, and is_ipv4_mapped_dangerous to a dedicated
registry_http module. Re-export under cfg\(feature = "transport-http-client"\).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(mcp-hub\): extract stateful pool from registry.rs

Move StatefulServerPool, StatefulPoolInner, LeaseTracker, and
LeaseState to a dedicated registry_pool module. Registry.rs reduced
from 911 to 641 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(weave\): extract git/fs operations from package.rs to package_git.rs

Moves CAS cache management, git clone/fetch/checkout, lockfile path
helpers, directory copy, SKILL.md detection, and store helpers into a
dedicated package_git.rs module. Reduces package.rs from 950 to 697
lines \(well under the 800-line soft limit\).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(cli\): extract SessionCommands from cli.rs to cli_session.rs

Reduces cli.rs from 958 to 790 lines \(below 800-line module size limit\).
Follows existing pattern of cli_todo.rs extraction using #[path] module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor\(pr-codex-bot\): extract rebase-clean-history to references/

Extract the Step 10.5 \(Rebase for Clean History\) detailed bash scripts and
inline fix cycles from PATTERN.md to references/rebase-clean-history.md.
PATTERN.md retains the summary, gate logic description, and link to the
reference file. Reduces PATTERN.md from 8476 to 5685 tokens \(under 8000 limit\).
workflow.toml is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(csa\): add pre-review quality gate, project hooks, and configurable timeout

Add a pre-review quality gate that runs `just pre-commit` before review/debate
sessions to catch issues early. Introduce project-level hooks configuration in
.csa/config.toml with pre_review, post_review, and pre_commit hook support.

Key changes:
- pipeline_gate: new module implementing quality gate with configurable retries,
  pass-through mode, and structured error reporting
- review_cmd_resolve: extract review argument resolution into dedicated module,
  reducing review_cmd.rs from god-object to focused orchestrator
- config: add ProjectHooksConfig and ReviewConfig.timeout field with global/project
  merge support and is_default\(\) for serde-default safety
- debate_cmd: integrate quality gate with debate workflow
- Comprehensive test coverage \(334 lines of gate tests, 452 lines of merge tests\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(do sed -i 's/vcs: Default::default\(\),/vcs: Default::default\(\),\\n            filesystem_sandbox: Default::default\(\),/' "$f")

Bash(JUST_TEMPDIR=/tmp/just git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(csa-config\): add tool output compression settings to SessionConfig

Add `tool_output_compression` \(bool, default false\) and
`tool_output_threshold_bytes` \(u64, default 8192\) fields to
SessionConfig. Extract SessionConfig, HooksSection, ExecutionConfig,
and VcsConfig into config_session.rs to stay under 800-line limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(CARGO_ADVISORY_DB_DIR=/tmp/advisory-dbs JUST_TEMPDIR=/tmp/just git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
feat\(csa-config\): add tool output compression settings to SessionConfig

Add `tool_output_compression` \(bool, default false\) and
`tool_output_threshold_bytes` \(u64, default 8192\) fields to
SessionConfig. Extract SessionConfig, HooksSection, ExecutionConfig,
and VcsConfig into config_session.rs to stay under 800-line limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(cli\): wire tool output compression into production execution path

should_compress_output\(\) and ToolOutputStore were defined but never
called from production code. Integrate them into pipeline_session_exec
after process_execution_result completes — this preserves the full
output for token parsing and hooks while replacing the caller-facing
output with a compact placeholder when compression is enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

hooks.command: case "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" in *"git push"*" main"*|*"git push"*" dev"*|*"git push origin main"*|*"git push origin dev"*) echo "BLOCKED: Never push to main/dev directly. Use feature branches + dev2merge." >&2; exit 1;; esac
